questions.question: In ancient Egypt, a survey of 100 pharaohs found that 60 liked $sphinx \cdot statues$ and 40 liked $mummy \cdot wraps$. If 20 pharaohs liked both, how many liked only $sphinx \cdot statues$?
A two-way table of artifacts from the ancient city of Pompeii shows that 15 out of 25 $vases$ had $glazed \cdot finishes$. What is the relative frequency of $vases$ with $glazed \cdot finishes$?

An association between $climate$ and $agriculture$ in ancient civilizations can be analyzed using two-way tables. If the table shows that 80% of civilizations with $hot \cdot climates$ practiced $irrigation$, what can be inferred?

options.A: Hot climates always lead to irrigation
options.B: Irrigation is more common in hot climates
options.C: Hot climates have no effect on irrigation
options.D: Irrigation is less common in hot climates
options.E: Other factors influence irrigation more than climate

questions.question: A two-way table of data from an archaeological site shows the number of $potsherds$ found in different $strata$. If 30 $potsherds$ were found in $stratum \ A$ and 20 were found in both $stratum \ A$ and $stratum \ B$, what is the number of $potsherds$ found only in $stratum \ A$?

The ancient Greeks used two-way tables to analyze the relationship between $olympic \ events$ and $athlete \ participation$. If 40 athletes participated in the $javelin \ throw$ and 20 also participated in the $discus \ throw$, what percentage of $javelin \ throw$ athletes also participated in the $discus \ throw$?

A museum has a collection of ancient $coins$ with different $denominations$. A two-way table shows that 50 $coins$ have a $denomination$ of $1 \ drachma$ and 20 have a $denomination$ of both $1 \ drachma$ and $2 \ drachma$. How many $coins$ have only a $denomination$ of $1 \ drachma$?

The construction of the ancient $pyramids$ required the use of $materials$ like $limestone$ and $granite$. A two-way table shows the number of $pyramids$ built with each $material$. If 15 $pyramids$ were built with $limestone$ and 5 were built with both $limestone$ and $granite$, what is the percentage of $pyramids$ built with only $limestone$?

A historical study of the $black \ death$ found an association between $social \ class$ and $survival \ rate$. If 60% of the $lower \ class$ survived and 20% of the $upper \ class$ survived, what can be inferred about the relationship between $social \ class$ and $survival \ rate$?

options.A: Social class has no effect on survival rate
options.B: Lower class has a higher survival rate
options.C: Upper class has a higher survival rate
options.D: Other factors influence survival rate more than social class

questions.question: Create a two-way table to analyze the relationship between $ancient \ civilization$ and $writing \ system$. Include at least 5 civilizations and 3 writing systems. Calculate the relative frequencies for each cell in the table.

A two-way table shows the number of $artifacts$ found in different $archaeological \ sites$. Analyze the association between $site \ location$ and $artifact \ type$. Provide examples of how the relative frequencies can be used to draw conclusions about the relationship between these variables.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Review the learning objectives for Unit 9 to ensure understanding of two-way tables
Tip 2: Use real-world historical examples to illustrate the concepts of relative frequency and association
Tip 3: Encourage students to use LaTeX commands to format their math responses
